recent_calls.summary: The user, Wesley Gillespie, called to schedule an emergency service to check the pilot light on his furnace. The agent successfully booked an appointment for 3 PM the same day and confirmed the details with the user. The user declined to provide feedback at the end of the call.

Judith called to confirm that she wants to keep her original cooling system check-up appointment scheduled for tomorrow between 10 and 12. The agent confirmed the details and offered information about maintenance programs, which Judith declined. The call ended with Judith expressing a preference not to talk to a machine and declining to provide feedback.

The user, Stephanie, called to schedule a replacement for both her heating and cooling units due to an air conditioner leaking coolant and a furnace with a cracked heat exchanger. The agent confirmed the details and provided available appointment slots for the next day, noting the user's timing preference as flexible. The call ended with the agent planning to pass the information along for follow-up confirmation.

The user called to inquire about a scheduled tune-up service for their conditioning unit that they had not yet heard about. The agent identified the request as a routine follow-up and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user, Terica from American Home Shield, called and requested to speak with a live representative. The agent, Sophie, acknowledged the request and successfully transferred the call to a live agent.

The user called to inquire about a technician visit scheduled for the day, specifically for air conditioner service. The agent attempted to gather more details, but the user mentioned checking a voicemail and decided to call back later. The call ended with the user hanging up.

The user called to check on a plumbing service appointment for a leaking faucet scheduled between 8 and 9 AM. The agent confirmed the details and informed the user that the appointment might be running late, then transferred the call to a live customer service agent for further assistance.

The user, Keith Weaver, called to discuss an appointment related to his American Home Shield home warranty service. He provided his service address and mentioned issues with the furnace and washer/dryer. The call ended with the user requesting to cancel the appointment due to a previous service issue, and the agent transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to inquire about the plumber's arrival time. The agent confirmed the appointment details, informing the user that the technician Rich is scheduled to arrive between 8 and 9 AM. The user had no further questions and ended the call politely.
The user called to inquire about billing. The agent informed the user that they do not have direct access to billing information and transferred the call to the billing team for assistance.

The user called to inquire about radon system installation, but the agent informed them that the company does not install radon systems and focuses on HVAC and related services. The user had no further questions and ended the call.

Gerard McClure called to reschedule his air conditioner service appointment because the key broke off in the lock, preventing technician access. The appointment was rescheduled for Friday, and Gerard confirmed his contact details for updates. He provided positive feedback about the helpfulness of the assistant.
The call was initiated by the user but ended abruptly without any interaction or information exchange, as the user did not respond to the agent's request for a ZIP code.
The call was initiated by the user who had a question, but the conversation was cut short as the user hung up before the agent could assist further.

Nicole Wells called to inquire about a quote for air conditioning maintenance service. The agent collected her contact and service address details but could not provide pricing after hours. Nicole decided to call back during business hours for pricing information.

The user, Derek, called to schedule a plumbing service for the installation of a Halo ion water treatment and tank replacement. The agent confirmed the details and noted the user's concern about the estimate's expiration and pricing. The agent assured Derek that the team would follow up on the next business day to confirm details and address his concerns.

The call was from Ruth at American Home Shield regarding a dispatch issue where a customer paid for a line set repair but was being asked to pay again. The agent confirmed the details and assured a follow-up update and invoice within 24 hours. The user declined information about maintenance programs and provided positive feedback on the call experience.

The user, Dr. Macklin, the building principal at Hazelwood Central High School, called to invite the recipient to a community night event on May 7th, highlighting student projects and performances. The call was informational and ended after the invitation was delivered.
The call was very brief and the agent attempted to engage the user, but the user did not respond and hung up shortly after.

The call was between Sophie from Budget and King from American Home Shield regarding a service request for James Harris III. King informed Sophie that the AC unit was added to the existing service request due to thermostat issues, and scheduling would be coordinated directly with Mr. Harris. The call concluded with Sophie confirming details and offering maintenance program information, which King declined.

The user called to confirm a service appointment for their heater, which was running too hot and causing concern. The agent gathered details and scheduled a technician to come out as soon as possible. The user confirmed the information and ended the call.

The user called Budget asking to speak with Danielle, but the agent Sophie informed them that Danielle was unavailable and offered assistance. The call ended shortly after without further requests or actions.

Cristen Firestone called to confirm the arrival time of the technician for the Panda Express in Chesterfield. The agent found the appointment scheduled for today and informed her, but Cristen clarified that the appointment was supposed to be next week and requested to speak with someone in the office. The call was then transferred to a live agent for further assistance.

The user, Deborah Parks, called to confirm her appointment scheduled for today with technician Brooks between 12 and 1 PM. The agent confirmed the appointment details and attempted to check if the technician was running late by transferring the call to a live agent. The call ended with a successful transfer.

The user called to confirm an appointment with Devon scheduled for the next day. The agent verified the appointment details, but the user indicated the information was incorrect. The call was then transferred to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called Budget and was prompted multiple times to provide their five-digit service address ZIP code but only pressed 0 repeatedly. The user then requested to speak with a live agent, and the call was successfully transferred to a live agent for further assistance.
The agent attempted to engage the user by requesting their service address ZIP code, but the user did not respond and ended the call shortly after.

The user called to inquire about a scheduled appointment for the day, expressing concern that the technician had not arrived by the expected time. The agent confirmed the appointment details and attempted to transfer the call to a live agent for further assistance. The call ended with a successful transfer to another agent.
The user, William, was unable to communicate clearly and decided to call back the next day. The agent, Sophie, offered assistance and left the conversation open for future contact.

The user, Paula Caldwell, called to verify the legitimacy of a suspicious letter regarding a home warranty allegedly secured by GoodLeap. The agent acknowledged the concern, explained they couldn't access billing details, and transferred the call to the billing team for further assistance. The user provided contact information and expressed worry about potential fraud.
The user requested to speak with a customer service representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was completed successfully without any issues.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent acknowledged the request and completed the transfer without issue.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, acknowledged the request and completed a successful call transfer.

Steve called to reschedule his appointment from Friday to Monday for an evaporator replacement on his cooling system. The agent confirmed the details and informed Steve that the request would be passed to the team for confirmation. The call ended with the agent asking for feedback.

Crystal called to schedule a home warranty service appointment for an electrical issue with her air conditioner at 447 Terry Drive, Columbia, Illinois. The agent confirmed details, including the home warranty dispatch number, and Crystal declined information about maintenance programs. She provided positive feedback, rating the experience as a 5 for its quickness.
The user, Pamela, requested to speak with a live agent during the call. The initial agent, Sophie, acknowledged the request and transferred the call to another agent for further assistance.
The user requested to speak to a live agent, and the initial agent transferred the call to another representative successfully. The call ended shortly after the transfer was completed.

The user called to inquire about an inspection scheduled between 1:00 and 2:30 at 1204 Iron Street. The agent asked for more details about the type of service for the inspection, but the call ended abruptly before the user could respond.
The user called and requested to speak with Nicole. The agent attempted to connect the user and successfully transferred the call to the appropriate agent.

Haley called to reschedule her air conditioner inspection appointment to Thursday at 1 PM at 1204 Iron Street. The agent confirmed the details and assured Haley that the team would reach out to confirm the new appointment. Haley was satisfied and ended the call politely.

Michael from American Home Shield called to cancel a work order for a tune-up for homeowner Blair Flowers. The agent confirmed the details and received positive feedback from Michael about the fast service. The call ended successfully with no further requests.
The user requested a return call and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance. The transfer was successful and the call ended shortly after.

Wayne from American Home Shield called to follow up on the status of a second opinion air conditioning system work order that was marked completed without a diagnosis. The agent confirmed details and informed Wayne that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact him shortly. Wayne declined information about maintenance programs and did not provide an email for follow-up.
The user requested to speak to a live agent, and the initial agent transferred the call to another agent successfully. The call lasted 16 seconds and ended with a call transfer.

The user called to discuss an estimate and requested to speak to a human agent. The initial agent transferred the call to a live agent to assist further. The call was successfully transferred without any issues.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the call was promptly transferred to another agent for further assistance.

Loretta called to reschedule her air conditioning service appointment originally scheduled for today, as she was stuck at work. The agent confirmed the details and informed her that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would follow up to confirm the new appointment time. Loretta declined information about maintenance programs and provided positive feedback about the interaction, though she expected to speak to a human.

The user, Sierra Johnson, called to inquire about the arrival time of technician Patrick. The agent confirmed the appointment details, informing Sierra that Patrick is scheduled to arrive between 9 AM and 10 AM. The user had no further requests and ended the call.
